slides.title: Clenching your jaw is *aging your face*.
slides.sub: Chasing a sharper jaw, many people are tensing theirs all day. A clenched jaw widens and strains the face.

slides.title: Grinding bulks the jaw muscle
slides.body: Nighttime grinding overworks the masseter, leaving a *wider, tense lower face* and morning headaches.

slides.title: Daytime clenching tracks stress
slides.body: You clench when you're stressed without noticing. *Catch it and unclench* throughout the day.

slides.title: Better sleep eases grinding
slides.body: Poor sleep worsens grinding. Sleep better, and ask a dentist about a *night guard* to protect teeth.

slides.title: Eat for relaxed muscles
slides.body: *Magnesium-rich foods* like greens and nuts may ease muscle tension. Food first — minor, and worth a try.

slides.title: Cut the evening stimulants
slides.body: *Late caffeine and alcohol* both worsen grinding. Move them earlier and your jaw rests overnight.

slides.title: A relaxed jaw, *tracked nightly*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the sleep, food and habits that calm a clenched jaw — in one daily score.

caption:
Chasing a sharper jaw, a lot of people are clenching theirs all day — which actually widens and strains the face. 😬

Unclench during the day, sleep better, ask a dentist about a guard, eat your greens, cut late caffeine and booze. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks the sleep, food and habits that calm it → link in bio.
youtube_title: How Clenching Your Jaw Is Quietly Aging Your Face
